methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
CarbonCure Technologies is a Series E-stage climate and energy company that has raised $80M+ to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector comprises 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the narrow peer set—filtered by stage, valuation tier, and business proximity—yields insufficient data (0 same-stage comparables, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier) to support a robust peer-median approach.

Consequently, the valuation relies on stage-progression analysis. Using the $80M total raised as a proxy for current implied value, a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement yields $112M. A sensitivity range around this central estimate, accounting for modest downside and upside variance, supports a band of $95M (low) to $129M (high).

The $112M median estimate is grounded in a single method and remains sensitive to the base assumption that prior capital raised equals current valuation—a rough proxy when external price discovery is limited. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent third-party valuations, limited Stage E comparables in the narrow peer set, and the sector's exposure to policy and technology development risk. Investors should treat this range as preliminary and contingent on deeper diligence around unit economics, customer concentration, and capital efficiency.
